seo.title: Divorce Lawyer in Lahore | Talaq, Khula & Mutual Divorce — Saeed Law Firm
seo.description: Experienced divorce lawyer in Lahore handling talaq, khula, and contested divorce at Lahore Family Courts. Confidential consultation. 50+ years' practice.

hero.intro: Experienced divorce lawyer in Lahore handling talaq, khula, and contested divorce at Lahore Family Courts. Confidential consultation. 50+ years' practice.

bodyMarkdown:
A divorce in Pakistan is not one process but three, each governed by a different legal mechanism. The right **divorce lawyer in Lahore** begins by identifying which path applies to your circumstances — talaq, khula, or mutual consent — because the procedure, timeline, and financial consequences differ at every step. **Saeed Law Firm** has handled divorce matters in Lahore since 1975, with appearances at the Family Courts at Aiwan-e-Adal, Mall Road, Union Council reconciliation proceedings, and appeals at the Lahore High Court. Our office at 13 Fane Road is 0.8 km from the family court complex.

### Three Types of Divorce Under Pakistani Law

**Talaq** is divorce pronounced by the husband under Section 7 of the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance 1961. After pronouncement, written notice must be served on the Union Council, which initiates a 90-day reconciliation period. The divorce becomes effective on expiry of those 90 days unless reconciliation succeeds. A **talaq lawyer in Lahore** handles the notice service, attendance at the reconciliation council, and post-divorce documentation including NADRA registration and recovery of haq mehr.

**Khula** is divorce sought by the wife through the Family Court under the Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act 1939. The grounds are listed in Section 2 of the Act and include cruelty, failure to maintain, desertion, impotence, and other recognised categories. Our [khula lawyer in Lahore](/khula-lawyer-in-lahore) page covers the procedure in detail.

**Mutual consent divorce** (mubarat) is the fastest path: a joint application to the Family Court, brief reconciliation effort, and decree. It typically completes within 90–120 days and is the preferred route where both spouses agree on dissolution and ancillary matters.

### Where Divorce Cases Are Heard in Lahore

The **Family Courts at Aiwan-e-Adal**, Mall Road, Lahore have exclusive jurisdiction over dissolution matters under the Schedule to the West Pakistan Family Courts Act 1964. The Union Council handles the talaq notice and reconciliation under Section 7 MFLO. Appeals from the Family Court go to the District Court, with further constitutional remedies at the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court).

### Divorce Procedure in Lahore — Step by Step

1. **Pronouncement or filing.** Talaq pronounced (oral or written) or khula petition filed at the Family Court.
2. **Union Council notice (talaq only).** Written notice served by the husband within prescribed time.
3. **Reconciliation period.** 90-day Union Council reconciliation effort for talaq; mandatory pre-trial reconciliation at Family Court for khula.
4. **Effective date / decree.** Talaq becomes effective after 90 days; khula decree issued after evidence and arguments.
5. **NADRA registration.** Divorce registered with NADRA for record updating.
6. **Ancillary recovery.** Haq mehr, dowry, and maintenance during iddat period.

### Best Divorce Lawyer in Lahore — How to Choose

The phrase **best divorce lawyer in Lahore** appears on dozens of directory listings, but the real criteria are simpler than they sound. Look for: (1) family-law specialisation, not generalist civil practice; (2) admission to the Lahore High Court for appeals; (3) confidential consultation environment with female support staff if needed; (4) a written case scope before engagement; (5) honest timeline expectations — no lawyer can guarantee a 30-day divorce, and any who promise this are violating Punjab Bar Council ethics.

### Realistic Timelines

| Type | Timeline |
|---|---|
| Talaq with Union Council notice | 90 days minimum (statutory) |
| Mutual consent divorce | 90–120 days |
| Uncontested khula | 6–9 months |
| Contested khula with custody/maintenance | 9–18 months |
| Appeal to District Court | additional 6–12 months |

### Recovery of Haq Mehr and Dowry

Recovery of dower (haq mehr) follows different rules depending on the type of divorce. In talaq, the wife retains dower as a matter of right. In khula, the wife typically returns the dower in exchange for the divorce decree, unless the grounds (such as cruelty) justify otherwise. Dowry articles are recoverable separately under the Dowry and Bridal Gifts (Restriction) Act 1976. Our **divorce lawyer Lahore** team files the recovery suits as part of dissolution proceedings or as separate suits depending on strategy.

### Maintenance During Iddat

A divorced wife is entitled to maintenance during the iddat period (approximately three months) under Pakistani family law. Recovery is enforced by the Family Court through monthly orders backed by attachment proceedings if non-payment occurs.

### Fees

- **Initial consultation:** PKR 5,000–10,000 with written case scope
- **Talaq with Union Council attendance:** fixed package
- **Mutual consent divorce:** fixed fee
- **Uncontested khula:** fixed fee
- **Contested matters:** stage-based (filing, evidence, defence, arguments, decree)
- **Recovery suits and execution:** separate engagement

### Why Saeed Law Firm

50+ years of practice in Lahore family courts. 800+ matters handled. Bilal Saeed, Advocate, personally reviews every divorce case. Office at 13 Fane Road, 0.8 km from Aiwan-e-Adal. Confidential consultation environment. Same-week scheduling for new clients.

### Frequently Asked Questions

**How long does a divorce take in Lahore family courts?**
Talaq with Union Council notice completes in 90 days minimum. Mutual divorce takes 90–120 days. Contested khula at the Lahore Family Courts ranges from 6–18 months depending on evidence and adjournments.

**What is the difference between talaq and khula?**
Talaq is divorce pronounced by the husband under MFLO 1961 Section 7; the wife retains haq mehr. Khula is wife-initiated dissolution under the Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act 1939 filed at the Family Court; the wife typically returns the dower.

**Do I have to attend Union Council reconciliation?**
Yes for talaq. The Union Council reconciliation is statutory under MFLO Section 7. Khula reconciliation is conducted by the Family Court itself.

**Can I get custody of children after divorce in Lahore?**
Custody is decided separately by the Guardian Court under the Guardians and Wards Act 1890. The welfare of the minor is the paramount consideration; for young children, custody typically vests with the mother.

**How is dowry recovered after divorce in Lahore?**
Dowry articles are recovered under the Dowry and Bridal Gifts (Restriction) Act 1976. The suit can be filed as part of dissolution proceedings or separately at the Family Court.

**How much does a divorce lawyer in Lahore charge?**
Initial consultation is PKR 5,000–10,000. Uncontested matters are typically fixed-fee; contested cases are billed by stage. A written fee proposal is shared before engagement.

### Related Lahore practice pages

A divorce lawyer Lahore engagement frequently extends into custody, maintenance, and dower-recovery work. See our [khula lawyer in Lahore](/khula-lawyer-in-lahore), [child custody lawyer in Lahore](/child-custody-lawyer-in-lahore), [court marriage lawyer in Lahore](/court-marriage-lawyer-in-lahore), and parent [family lawyer in Lahore](/family-lawyer-in-lahore) pages. Most cases are filed at the [Family Courts at Aiwan-e-Adal](/courts/family-court-lawyer-lahore); appeals reach the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court). When choosing the best divorce lawyer in Lahore, look for family-law specialisation rather than general civil practice — divorce lawyer Lahore work has its own evidentiary and procedural rhythm. To meet Bilal Saeed, Advocate, [book a consultation](/contact-us).

faqs.q: How do I book a consultation with Saeed Law Firm?
faqs.a: Use the contact form on this site, call our Fane Road office, or WhatsApp us. We respond within one business day and schedule a same-week appointment for most matters.

faqs.q: Do you serve clients outside Lahore?
faqs.a: Yes. We represent clients across Pakistan and overseas Pakistanis, with primary court appearances in Lahore. Remote consultations are available via Zoom and WhatsApp.
